questions.question: Implement a 5-node Ethernet LAN with source node S0 and destination node D3. For each node, set the initial congestion window to 1 packet and the maximum congestion window to 5 packets. Plot the congestion window for each node as a function of the number of packets sent. How does the congestion window change for the source and destination nodes? Explain your answer in 20 words or less.
questions.hint: Use a graph to visualize the congestion window values

questions.question: An Ethernet LAN consists of 10 nodes, each with an initial congestion window of 2 packets and a maximum congestion window of 10 packets. Source node S0 sends packets to destination node D5. Plot the congestion window for S0 and D5 as a function of the number of packets sent. What is the average congestion window for S0 and D5? Explain your answer.
questions.hint: Use a graph to visualize the congestion window values

questions.question: A network simulator is used to model a 4-node Ethernet LAN with source node S0 and destination node D3. The initial congestion window for S0 is set to 3 packets and the maximum congestion window is set to 7 packets. The simulator plots the congestion window for each node as a function of the number of packets sent. What is the impact of increasing the packet size on the congestion window? Explain your answer in 50 words or less.
questions.hint: Use the plotted graph to answer the question
